Myeloid			    Lymphoid
Early step in adult hematopoiesis
Note:	can have myelogenous  or lymphocytic leukemia
acute or chronic

Red Blood Cells: Erythrocytes
Are packed with hemoglobin
Extrude their nuclei
Live ~120 days in a human body
Megakaryocytes produce platelets for blood clotting

Platelets are in blue
Aggregated Platelets

Blood cell lineages-Cells of the Immune system
Note:
This is not a complete list of the cells you need to know
Note:
leukocytes are white blood cells

3 Main Categories of Leucocytes
(White Blood Cells)
Granulocytes
Monocytes
Lymphocytes

Neutrophil                   Basophil                         Eosinophill
Granulocytes

Granulocytes
-All contain numerous lysosomes and secretory vesicles (or granules)
-All have mutlilobed nuclei
-3 categories
Neutrophils or polymorphonuclear leukocytes
-most common type of granulocyte
-Phagocytose and destroy small organisms 				especially bacteria
Basophils/Mast cells
-secrete histamine to help mediate inflammatory 			reactions
-Closely related
Eosinophils
help destroy parasites and modulate allergic 				inflammatory responses

Different Granulocytes are distinguished by staining characteristics
Stains with both acid and basic dyes         Stains with acid dye eosin red             Stains with basic dye methylene blue
Eosinophils swarming a worm
Macrophage

Monocytes (mononuclear phagocytes)
-once they leave the bloodstream, they mature into macrophages
-along with neutrophils are the main “professional phagocytes”
-macrophages contain specialized lysosomes that fuse with phagosomes and expose microorganisms to a barrage of enzymatically produced nasties like superoxide O2- and hypochlorite HOCl (the active ingredient in bleach), and a mixture of lysosomal hydrolases.
-much larger and longer lived than neutrophils.
-remove senescent, dead, and damaged cells, unique in being able to devour large microorganisms such as protozoa.
-macrophages have different names, depending on the tissue in which they are found
Comparison of Granulocyte (left) and Monocyte

Lymphocytes
-B Cells produce antibodies
-T Cells influence other cell types, and some can kill
-lymphocyte like cells called natural killer (NK) cells which kill 	some types of tumor cells and some virus infected cells
Note: plasma cell is a antibody secreting B cell

Blood cell lineages-Cells of the Immune system

This is not a complete list of the cells you need to know
Note:
leukocytes are white blood cells

Dendritic Cells

Dendritic Cells
-first cells of the immune system discovered
-covered with long membranous extensions that resemble dendrites of nerve cells
-arise from different pathways and in different locations
-very versatile
-are the main type of cells that drive T cells into activity
-sentinels
Many types of Dendritic cells and multiple origins

Note: there is a copy of ecopy of this on Google Drive that is easier to read. Saved as hematopoiesis.png
Note: ~ 10 cell types in blood
